name: Gold Fields Limited
reporting_currency: USD (US dollars); JSE-listed, ZAR share price & dividends
registered_address: 150 Helen Road, Sandown, Sandton, 2196, South Africa
operating_assets: South Deep (South Africa) - sole SA mine, ~90.2% owned, long-life mechanised
Tarkwa (Ghana) - large open-pit; mining leases expire April 2027 (renewal not automatic)
Damang (Ghana) - handed back to the State 18 April 2026
St Ives, Agnew, Granny Smith, Gruyere (Australia) - Gruyere 100% since Gold Road buy-out Oct 2025
Cerro Corona (Peru) - moving to stockpile/tailings processing; LOM to ~2031
Salares Norte (Chile) - commercial production Aug 2025; ultra-low-cost growth engine
Windfall (Canada) - ex-Osisko Mining (acq. Oct 2024); pre-build, FID guided mid-2026, first gold ~H1 2029

key_people.chairman: John MacKenzie (from May 2026; succeeded Yunus Suleman)

notes: USD reporter, Dec year-end. FY2025 record: revenue US$8,751m, adjusted EBITDA US$5,637m, attributable profit US$3,567m (incl. ~US$808m one-off Gruyere gain), normalised profit US$2,684m, AISC US$1,645/oz, attributable production 2.438Moz, adjusted FCF US$2,970m, net debt 0.26x EBITDA. FY2026 guidance: 2.40-2.60Moz, AISC US$1,800-2,000/oz, capex US$1.9-2.1bn. Statutory identifiers (ISIN/registration/LEI) left null pending verification. Identify Gold Fields != Gold Road Resources (acquired) and != GoldFields the US property firm.
download_note: 2026-06-17 (quick mode): FY2025 ORIGINAL binaries verified REACHABLE but NOT saved to disk in this environment. AFS PDF (afs-2025.pdf) resolves 200 / application/pdf / 8,899,236 bytes / %PDF magic; FY2025 results presentation (reports/q4-2026/pdf/presentation.pdf) resolves 200 / application/pdf / 6,843,798 bytes. Binary save blocked by TWO environment limits: (1) workspace bash egress is an allowlist proxy that 403s ALL external domains (X-Proxy-Error: blocked-by-allowlist, confirmed vs goldfields.com, sec.gov, example.com), so curl/requests cannot reach the file; (2) the browser CAN fetch the bytes (XHR returned full 8.9MB, head %PDF) but the tool harness blocks base64 payloads in tool results, so browser-to-disk transfer is also impossible. Not site-gating, not bypassable per instructions. FY2025 headline figures verified instead against the official reviewed-results microsite https://www.goldfields.com/reports/q4-2026/ .
coverage_gaps: FY2025 Annual Financial Statements ORIGINAL PDF outstanding - https://www.goldfields.com/pdf/investors/integrated-annual-reports/2025/afs-2025.pdf (8.9MB, verified 200/application/pdf/%PDF). Not saved: workspace egress allowlist 403s the domain AND harness blocks base64 transit from browser. Retry from an environment with direct outbound HTTP.
FY2025 Results Presentation ORIGINAL PDF outstanding - https://www.goldfields.com/reports/q4-2026/pdf/presentation.pdf (6.8MB, verified 200/application/pdf). Same two environment blocks.
Quick-mode skips (backfill on a deep run): FY2025 Integrated Annual Report PDF, H1 2025 interim, trading statements, full SENS/6-K history, prior-year (FY2024) documents.

Gold_Fields_GFI_12-18m_Outlook_Hormuz_Sc._provenance: Internal IC briefing: 12-18m outlook, management risks, management incentives, and Strait-of-Hormuz Scenarios A (open) vs B (closed). Built from GF FY2025 results, Q1 2026 update, and macro research (gold/Brent/Hormuz).

Gold_Fields_GFI_Scenario_Model_Jun2026.x._provenance: Formula-driven companion scenario model (gold price / AISC / FX -> Revenue, EBITDA, FCF) under Hormuz-open vs Hormuz-closed; calibrated to FY2025 actuals (98.3%).
